evidence: ExploreResponseAssembler is a clean single-purpose boundary translator: all model-to-JSON translation for exploration responses lives there, keeping ExplorationResult/ComparisonResult free of presentation concerns.
ExplorationService interface provides a stable, explicit seam between HTTP handlers and the exploration algorithm layer; ExplorationHandlers delegates cleanly without back-and-forth calls.
GrammarConfigLoader + GrammarConfig + GrammarConfigSerializer cleanly partition I/O, data, and presentation: each layer has one concern and depends only inward.
QueryExecutor default methods (executeSurfacePatternForRelation, executeBcqlQueryForRelation) centralise pattern-construction logic, keeping HTTP handlers free of RelationPatternBuilder calls.
HttpApiUtils.wrapWithErrorHandling provides uniform error propagation across all handler boundaries (IllegalArgumentException→400, IOException→500), with a single mapping point.

ConcordanceHandlers.handleConcordanceExamples calls grammarConfig.relation(req.relation()) without going through RelationUtils.resolveRelationAlias, so aliases like 'adj_predicate' silently fail to resolve there while working in ExplorationHandlers.resolveRelationConfig, which calls resolveRelationAlias before the same grammarConfig.relation() lookup.

ComparisonResult.fullyShared(), partiallyShared(), specific() re-implement filter predicates (presentInCount == nouns.size(), >= 2 and < size, == 1) that are already encapsulated in CollocateProfile.isFullyShared()/isPartiallyShared()/isSpecific(); summaryCounts() correctly delegates to sharingCategory() but the streaming accessors do not.

ConcordanceHandlers.toResultMap is a trivial one-liner that adds zero translation and exists only to alias ExploreResponseAssembler.collocateToExampleMap — adds navigational indirection with no benefit.

strengths: ExploreResponseAssembler centralises all model-to-presentation translation for exploration responses in one place, keeping model classes free of JSON concerns.
ExplorationService interface makes the HTTP-to-exploration seam explicit, testable, and stable; handlers never reach through to algorithm internals.
GrammarConfigLoader / GrammarConfig / GrammarConfigSerializer form a clean three-layer split: I/O, data, and serialization each own their concern with inward-only dependencies.
QueryExecutor default convenience methods keep BCQL pattern-construction out of HTTP handler code, avoiding duplicated RelationPatternBuilder calls at call sites.
HttpApiUtils.wrapWithErrorHandling provides a single, consistent error-propagation mapping point for all handler boundaries.
mid_level_elegance.issue_character: The issues are isolated rather than systemic: one concrete seam inconsistency (alias resolution absent at the concordance boundary) and two minor predicate-duplication cases; the overall boundary design is disciplined and the translation points are well-identified.
mid_level_elegance.score_rationale: The codebase scores solidly above 70 because the major boundaries — HTTP-to-exploration, model-to-JSON, config load/data/serialize — are explicit, minimal, and each translate in one obvious place. The score stops short of 85 primarily because of the alias-resolution gap between ConcordanceHandlers and ExplorationHandlers: a developer using identical 'relation' parameter values across sibling endpoints will see different behaviour with no warning, which is the defining failure mode of mid-level seam design. The predicate duplication in ComparisonResult is a secondary friction point that adds maintenance risk at the model boundary.

issues.summary: ConcordanceHandlers skips RelationUtils.resolveRelationAlias, so short aliases (e.g. 'adj_predicate') silently fail there but work on sibling explore endpoints
evidence: ConcordanceHandlers.parseConcordanceExamplesRequest stores params.getOrDefault("relation", "noun_adj_predicates") raw and passes it directly to grammarConfig.relation(req.relation()) with no alias resolution.

A caller passing relation=adj_predicate to /api/concordance/examples gets rel.isEmpty()==true and falls back to a proximity pattern (logged as a warning), while the same parameter to /api/semantic-field/explore resolves correctly to 'noun_adj_predicates'.
issues.suggestion: Extract alias resolution into a shared helper used by both handlers: add a package-private static method resolveRelation(Map<String,String> params, String defaultId) in HttpApiUtils or RelationUtils that applies resolveRelationAlias before the GrammarConfig lookup, and call it from both parseConcordanceExamplesRequest and resolveRelationConfig. This collapses the two divergent paths into one.

issues.summary: ComparisonResult.fullyShared/partiallyShared/specific() re-implement filter predicates already encapsulated in CollocateProfile, creating a dual-definition boundary seam
evidence: ComparisonResult.fullyShared() filters by a.presentInCount() == nouns.size(); CollocateProfile.isFullyShared() checks presentInCount == totalNouns — same predicate expressed twice.
ComparisonResult.partiallyShared() repeats a.presentInCount() >= 2 && a.presentInCount() < nouns.size(); CollocateProfile.isPartiallyShared() already encapsulates this.
ComparisonResult.summaryCounts() correctly delegates to c.sharingCategory(), so the same object has both the delegating and non-delegating paths; the stream accessors don't follow the same pattern.
issues.suggestion: Rewrite the three stream accessors to delegate to CollocateProfile's own predicates: fullyShared() -> collocates.stream().filter(CollocateProfile::isFullyShared), partiallyShared() -> filter(CollocateProfile::isPartiallyShared), specific() -> filter(CollocateProfile::isSpecific). This makes ComparisonResult consistent with summaryCounts() and ensures predicate changes propagate from one place.

root_causes: The concordance alias gap likely arose because ConcordanceHandlers was extracted from a different origin than ExplorationHandlers; the alias-resolution step was added to the exploration path but not back-ported to the concordance path.
ComparisonResult predicate drift reflects incremental addition of summaryCounts() (which used sharingCategory()) after the stream accessors were written, without updating the older methods to match.
likely_symptoms: concordance_alias_gap — the fallback proximity query path in ConcordanceHandlers is a symptom, not the root cause
